description: A designed bullet-point statistics card with a dark navy/slate background. In the upper right, faded into the background, is a photograph of President Trump in a dark suit and red 'GULF OF AMERICA' / '47' baseball cap, hands at his sides, walking on what appears to be an airport tarmac (Air Force One blurred behind him). The headline at top center is the single word 'Economy' in large white serif (Didot-style) type, underlined with a thin white rule. Below are five bulleted economic talking points; the first and last bullets are highlighted in bright yellow text while the middle three are white: 'The U.S. economy added 115k jobs in April, including 123k private sector jobs, smashing expectations.'; 'Private sector weekly earnings up a massive 5.6% in April.'; 'U.S. Steel announces $1.9 billion investment in Arkansas manufacturing facility.'; 'Core U.S. factory orders grew 4.09% year-over-year in March, highest since Nov. 2022.'; 'White House: President Trump's drug pricing policy will save the U.S. economy $529 billion over the next decade.' A small white White House building icon is centered at the bottom as the official seal/branding. The composition mimics a premium news/financial graphic to project credibility; the propaganda technique is a curated list of favorable economic statistics attributed to the administration. Target audience: general public and supporters, framing the economy as booming under Trump.
notable_text: Economy; added 115k jobs in April; weekly earnings up a massive 5.6%; U.S. Steel $1.9 billion investment; drug pricing policy will save $529 billion over the next decade
